title: warbeast2000 npm Package Steals Developer SSH Private Keys and Uploads to Attacker GitHub Repo
description: The malicious npm package warbeast2000 ran a postinstall script that read the private SSH key stored in ~/.ssh/id_rsa, base64-encoded it, and uploaded it to a GitHub repository controlled by the attacker, compromising developer authentication credentials across 412 downloads.
label: AV-400

title: kodiak2k npm Package Invokes Mimikatz Tool to Extract Credentials from Developer Systems
description: The malicious npm package kodiak2k executed a postinstall script that fetched and ran the Mimikatz credential-extraction tool on victim developer machines, harvesting SSH keys and Windows authentication credentials across more than 1,281 downloads before removal.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious RubyGems Targeting South Korean Developers Exfiltrate Credentials via Social Media Automation Tools
description: A threat actor published 60 malicious RubyGems posing as automation tools for Instagram, Twitter, TikTok, and Kakao that presented a GUI requesting login credentials and silently exfiltrated usernames and passwords to attacker-controlled servers targeting primarily South Korean users.
label: AV-400

title: Malicious Go Module Appends Hardcoded SSH Public Key to authorized_keys for Persistent Backdoor Access
description: Malicious Go modules in the BufferZoneCorp campaign appended a hardcoded SSH public key to victim systems' ~/.ssh/authorized_keys file, establishing persistent backdoor access to compromised build servers without requiring further credentials after initial execution.
label: AV-400

title: litellm PyPI Package Compromised with Double Base64-Encoded Payload Exfiltrating All Environment Variables and SSH Keys
description: Malicious versions of the litellm PyPI package contained a double base64-encoded payload that evaded static analysis and exfiltrated all environment variables, SSH keys, and cloud credentials to an attacker-controlled server, with PyPI admins quarantining the project after discovery.
label: AV-400

title: Compromised Checkmarx KICS Docker Images Harvest GitHub Tokens AWS and Azure Credentials
description: Official Checkmarx KICS Docker images and VS Code extensions were compromised with an obfuscated payload that harvested GitHub authentication tokens, AWS credentials, Azure and Google Cloud tokens, npm configuration files, and SSH keys before compressing and encrypting the data for exfiltration.
label: AV-400

title: xinference PyPI Package Versions Carry Credential-Stealing Payload Targeting SSH Keys and Crypto Wallets
description: Three consecutive releases of xinference on PyPI carried a credential-stealing payload attributed to the TeamPCP threat group that decoded a second-stage collector harvesting SSH keys, cloud credentials, environment variables, and cryptocurrency wallets before transmitting them to a C2 server.
label: AV-400

title: sonatype npm Package Identified as Fully Compromising Any Installed System
description: The sonatype npm package was identified as fully malicious, with the GitHub Security Advisory warning that any computer with the package installed or running should be considered completely compromised, requiring immediate rotation of all secrets and keys from a separate machine.
label: AV-400

title: pgserve npm Package Injects Credential-Harvesting Script via postinstall Hook on Every Install
description: Malicious versions of pgserve, a PostgreSQL server package for Node.js, injected a credential-harvesting script that executed via a postinstall hook on every npm install, silently collecting developer credentials from all systems where the package was installed.
label: AV-400

title: S1ngularity Attack Steals Nx npm Token via GitHub Actions Injection to Publish Malicious Packages
description: Attackers exploited a GitHub Actions injection vulnerability in the Nx repository to steal the npm publish token, using it to release malicious Nx package versions that scanned developer systems for sensitive data and attempted to leverage local AI tools including Claude and Gemini before uploading results.
label: AV-400

title: aliyun-ai-labs-snippets-sdk PyPI Package Typosquats Legitimate Aliyun AI SDK to Steal Credentials
description: Malicious PyPI packages aliyun-ai-labs-snippets-sdk and aliyun-ai-labs-sdk impersonated the legitimate Aliyun AI Labs Python SDK, establishing a C2 server connection and exfiltrating sensitive environment variables and configuration information from developer machines within 24 hours of publication.
label: AV-200

title: Typosquatted PyPI Packages Target Bittensor Crypto Wallets by Hijacking Staking Functions
description: Malicious typosquatted PyPI packages targeting the Bittensor blockchain network hijacked staking functions through monkey-patching, intercepting private keys each time a keypair was generated and transmitting them to attacker infrastructure to drain cryptocurrency wallets.
label: AV-200

title: activesupport-logger Malicious RubyGem Typosquats Rails activesupport to Steal AWS and SSH Credentials
description: A malicious RubyGem named activesupport-logger typosquatted the popular Rails activesupport library, harvesting environment variables, SSH keys in ~/.ssh/id_rsa and ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, AWS credentials, .npmrc tokens, and GitHub CLI configuration before exfiltrating as JSON to a remote endpoint.
label: AV-200

title: devise-jwt Malicious RubyGem Typosquats Authentication Library Targeting Ruby on Rails Applications
description: A malicious RubyGem typosquatting the devise-jwt authentication library executed credential theft during install time via extconf.rb, harvesting developer authentication secrets and API tokens from Ruby on Rails applications before the package was reported and removed.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious Go Module Typosquats HashiCorp go-retryablehttp to Poison GOPROXY and Steal Credentials
description: A malicious Go module impersonating HashiCorp's go-retryablehttp modified GITHUB_ENV, poisoned GOPROXY settings, weakened checksum protections, and tampered with go.sum to intercept downstream dependency resolution while stealing developer and CI environment credentials.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious Go Module Impersonates Kelsey Hightower envconfig to Plant Fake Go Wrapper in CI Path
description: A malicious Go module impersonating Kelsey Hightower's envconfig planted a fake go executable in a cache directory and appended it to the workflow path, causing the wrapper to be selected before the real binary in CI environments and enabling arbitrary command execution.
label: AV-200

title: semantic-types PyPI Package Ships Benign Version Before Introducing Malicious Payload 35 Days Later
description: The semantic-types PyPI package was initially benign when uploaded on December 22, 2024, but a malicious credential-stealing payload was introduced as an update on January 26, 2025, exploiting developer trust in the package's clean installation history to evade security review.
label: AV-200

title: lightning PyPI Package Versions 2.6.2 and 2.6.3 Execute Credential-Stealing Malware on Import
description: Socket detected that lightning PyPI package versions 2.6.2 and 2.6.3 executed credential-stealing malware upon import rather than installation, targeting the PyTorch Lightning machine learning framework's user base of AI researchers and data scientists.
label: AV-200

title: Compromised Namastex.ai npm Packages Use ICP Canister as Decentralized C2 Channel for Credential Theft
description: Compromised Namastex.ai npm packages used an Internet Computer Protocol canister as a resilient decentralized command-and-control channel, enabling credential theft with self-propagation logic that continued operating even when traditional C2 infrastructure was taken down.
label: AV-200

title: cappership PyPI Packages Monkey-Patch Solana Key Generation to Intercept Every Generated Private Key
description: PyPI packages published by threat actor cappership monkey-patched Solana key-generation methods at runtime without modifying source code, capturing every private key generated by affected applications and exfiltrating them to attacker infrastructure while providing polished README files and linked GitHub repositories for credibility.
label: AV-200

title: Malicious Go Module Poisons GOPROXY Setting to Redirect All Dependency Resolution Through Attacker Proxy
description: A malicious Go module in the BufferZoneCorp campaign modified the GOPROXY environment variable to redirect all subsequent Go dependency resolution through an attacker-controlled proxy server, enabling man-in-the-middle attacks on all future dependency downloads in the compromised environment.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious Go Module Tampers with go.sum Checksum Database to Weaken Dependency Integrity Verification
description: Malicious Go modules tampered with go.sum checksum entries and weakened GONOSUMCHECK protections, making downstream dependency resolution easier to intercept and subvert by undermining the integrity verification mechanisms that protect Go module downloads.
label: AV-509

title: paranoid2 RubyGem with 100K+ Downloads Identified as Malicious Supply Chain Attack (CVE-2019-13589)
description: The paranoid2 RubyGem with over 100,000 downloads was identified as a supply chain attack and assigned CVE-2019-13589, demonstrating that malicious packages can achieve massive scale in production environments before detection and removal by registry maintainers.
label: AV-509

title: simple_captcha2 RubyGem with 100K+ Downloads Confirmed as Malware (CVE-2019-14282)
description: The simple_captcha2 RubyGem with over 100,000 downloads was confirmed as malicious and assigned CVE-2019-14282, illustrating how dependency confusion and supply chain attacks can affect extremely widely-used packages before security researchers detect and report them.
label: AV-509

title: datagrid RubyGem Supply Chain Attack Affects 100K+ Downloads and Receives CVE-2019-14281
description: The datagrid RubyGem was identified as a supply chain attack affecting over 100,000 downloads, receiving CVE-2019-14281 and highlighting the scale of impact that malicious packages in widely-used ecosystems like RubyGems can achieve before detection.
label: AV-509

title: malicious-npm-package Runs PowerShell to Download and Execute Remote Executable on Windows Systems
description: The malicious-npm-package npm package targeted Windows systems by running a PowerShell command that downloaded an executable file from a remote attacker-controlled server and executed it, giving the attacker full control of any Windows developer system where the package was installed.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious Go Module grpc-client Impersonates Infrastructure Tooling to Steal CI Environment Secrets
description: A malicious Go module named grpc-client blended into normal dependency manifests by impersonating common infrastructure tooling names, executing through init() to detect CI environments and steal GITHUB_ENV, GITHUB_PATH, and pipeline secrets from compromised build systems.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious Go Module config-loader Sets HTTP_PROXY to Redirect All Traffic Through Attacker Infrastructure
description: A malicious Go module named config-loader set HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY environment variables to route all outbound traffic from compromised CI environments through attacker-controlled proxy infrastructure, enabling interception of sensitive API calls and credential transmission.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious Go Module go-stdlib-ext Writes Fake go Executable to Intercept All Go Commands in Workflows
description: A malicious Go module named go-stdlib-ext wrote a fake go executable binary into a cache directory and appended the directory to workflow PATH variables, causing all subsequent go commands in CI pipelines to be intercepted by the attacker-controlled wrapper instead of the real Go binary.
label: AV-509

title: Malicious RubyGems Systematically Exfiltrate from Eight Standard Developer Credential Paths
description: Malicious RubyGems in the BufferZoneCorp campaign systematically exfiltrated credentials from eight standard developer paths including ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.npmrc, ~/.gem/credentials, ~/.netrc, ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, and ~/.gitconfig before encoding as JSON for transmission.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters Embedded in GitHub Actions YAML Configuration Files Alter Pipeline Behavior
description: Security researchers documented Trojan Source attacks where hidden Unicode bidirectional control characters are embedded in GitHub Actions YAML configuration files, causing CI pipeline runners to interpret different steps, conditions, and secrets than what administrators see when reviewing the configuration.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Dockerfiles Cause Container Builds to Execute Hidden Malicious Commands
description: Trojan Source attacks documented in the original research demonstrate how invisible Unicode characters in Dockerfile RUN instructions cause container build systems to execute fundamentally different shell commands from what developers see during Dockerfile review.
label: AV-300

title: CVE-2021-42574 Affects Python Interpreter: Unicode Characters Alter Indentation-Sensitive Logic
description: CVE-2021-42574 is particularly severe for Python due to the language's indentation-sensitive syntax, where Unicode bidirectional characters can alter which code blocks are included in functions and conditionals, invisibly changing program logic in ways especially difficult to detect in code review.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Vulnerability Confirmed in Rust Compiler Affecting Memory Safety Guarantees
description: The Trojan Source research confirmed that the Rust compiler is vulnerable to Unicode bidirectional character attacks, potentially undermining Rust's memory safety guarantees by allowing hidden unsafe code blocks to be included in what appears to be safe Rust code during review.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Unicode Bidirectional Characters Affect Go Source Code Compilation and String Handling
description: The Go programming language compiler was confirmed vulnerable to Trojan Source attacks where Unicode bidirectional characters alter string literal interpretation and conditional logic during compilation, enabling hidden backdoors in Go server applications and infrastructure tools.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Vulnerability in Java: Unicode Characters Alter Access Modifier and Method Scope
description: The original Trojan Source research demonstrated that Java is vulnerable to Unicode bidirectional character attacks where hidden characters alter access modifier interpretation and method scope boundaries, potentially exposing private methods as public-accessible or hiding injected code in class definitions.
label: AV-300

title: CVE-2021-42694 Homoglyph Attack in C++ Enables Invisible Function Pointer Substitution
description: CVE-2021-42694 demonstrates a homoglyph Trojan Source attack in C++ where visually identical Unicode characters enable invisible function pointer substitution, causing security-critical callback functions to be replaced with attacker-controlled implementations invisible during code review.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters Alter SQL Query Logic to Bypass Authentication Checks
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in SQL where hidden Unicode characters alter query logic, causing WHERE clauses and JOIN conditions to evaluate differently from their visual representation, potentially bypassing authentication checks and data access controls.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Shell Scripts Redirect Output and Alter Command Execution
description: Trojan Source attacks in shell scripts use Unicode bidirectional characters to redirect command output, alter pipe handling, and change command substitution behavior, causing CI/CD pipeline scripts to execute entirely different commands from what system administrators see during script review.
label: AV-300

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Terraform Infrastructure Code Alter Cloud Resource Configurations
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Terraform infrastructure-as-code where hidden Unicode characters alter resource configurations, causing cloud infrastructure to be provisioned with different security groups, IAM policies, and network configurations from what DevOps engineers review.
label: AV-300

title: Checkmarx KICS Docker Hub Repository Compromised to Deliver Credential-Stealing Container Images
description: Docker Hub flagged suspicious activity on the checkmarx/kics repository where official container images were replaced with compromised versions harvesting GitHub tokens, AWS credentials, Azure tokens, npm configuration files, SSH keys, and environment variables from CI environments pulling the images.
label: AV-410

title: TeamPCP Threat Group Coordinates Simultaneous Supply Chain Attacks Across npm PyPI and Docker Hub
description: The TeamPCP threat group coordinated simultaneous supply chain attacks across npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub within 48 hours, compromising packages and container images used in CI/CD pipelines and demonstrating the increasing sophistication of multi-ecosystem supply chain attack campaigns.
label: AV-410

title: CanisterSprawl Supply Chain Campaign Uses Decentralized ICP Canister for Resilient C2 Infrastructure
description: The CanisterSprawl supply chain campaign tracked by Socket and StepSecurity used an Internet Computer Protocol canister as a decentralized and resilient C2 channel, making takedowns significantly harder than traditional centralized C2 infrastructure and enabling persistent campaign operations.
label: AV-410

title: BufferZoneCorp Malicious Go Modules Tamper with GitHub Actions Workflows via GITHUB_ENV Manipulation
description: Malicious Go modules in the BufferZoneCorp campaign executed through init() to detect GITHUB_ENV and GITHUB_PATH variables, manipulating workflow environment to intercept subsequent build steps and establish persistent unauthorized access to CI pipeline execution.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious RubyGems Exploit extconf.rb Native Extension Build Process for Install-Time Credential Theft
description: Malicious RubyGems exploited Ruby's native extension build process by weaponizing extconf.rb to execute credential-stealing code automatically during gem installation, running with elevated build permissions before any user invokes the package's advertised functionality.
label: AV-410

title: Automated Upgrade Bots Silently Merge Malicious Package Updates into Protected Branches Within Minutes
description: Supply chain attacks leveraging automated dependency upgrade bots such as Dependabot and Renovate were documented where malicious package updates were silently merged into protected branches within minutes of publication, with automation tools bypassing human review processes intended to catch supply chain compromises.
label: AV-410

title: S1ngularity Attack Bypasses npm Provenance Signing by Publishing Directly with Stolen Token
description: The S1ngularity supply chain attack against Nx bypassed npm provenance signing protections by using a stolen npm publish token to upload malicious packages directly, demonstrating that provenance signing does not prevent installation of unsigned packages published with compromised credentials.
label: AV-410

title: Malicious RubyGem Logger Runtime Path Steals Environment Data on First Logger Invocation
description: Malicious RubyGems implemented a runtime credential theft path wired into the custom logger class that sent environment data including tokens, API keys, and secrets to attacker infrastructure the first time the logger was invoked, combining install-time and runtime attack vectors.
label: AV-410

title: BufferZoneCorp Campaign Uses Sleeper Packages That Activate Malicious Payloads Only After Update
description: The BufferZoneCorp supply chain campaign published initial sleeper versions of Ruby gems and Go modules with plausible utility branding and clean behavior, then updated nearly every package to add active payloads including credential exfiltration, GitHub Actions tampering, and SSH persistence.
label: AV-410

title: npm Supply Chain Attack Achieves Cryptocurrency Wallet Drain Within 16 Minutes of Malicious Package Publication
description: A targeted npm supply chain attack documented by Sygnia achieved cryptocurrency wallet draining through traffic interception and transaction manipulation of browser-based crypto wallets within just 16 minutes of publishing malicious updates to widely-used packages.
label: AV-410
